sglang.title: feat: add original logprobs to response
sglang.author: narutolhy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR adds support for returning the original (pre-normalized) log probabilities of tokens in the sampling result. This can be useful for downstream applications that require access to raw model scores, such as reranking, calibration, or further custom postprocessing.

sglang.title: Adds initialize_moe_config to bench_one_batch so MOE backend is respected
sglang.author: pranavm-nvidia
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, the bench_one_batch script does not respect the MOE backend option and will always use the default backend. In other entrypoints (e.g. launch_server) this is not an issue because they most likely use the scheduler, which calls initialize_moe_config and therefore uses the correc

sglang.title: feature(eplb): add min-rebalancing-utilization-threshold for eplb
sglang.author: hzh0425
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
co-author: yizhang2077 1109276519@qq.com
Motivation
The primary intent of this PR is to address that EPLB triggers Expert Load Balancing every eplb_rebalance_num_iterations, incurring second-level latency. When GPU loads are inherently balanced, such LB activations become unnecessary.
Modifications

sglang.title: Make fp4_quantize kernels work on sm103
sglang.author: hlu1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The fp4_quantize kernels use cvt with .e2m1x2 instructions with guards that are sm100a exclusive, which make them fail on sm103a.
Modifications

Replace the macros that guard cvt with .e2m1x2 with more simpler cutlass macros for both sm_100a and sm_103a.
Move the common functions to heade

PD-Disaggregation decode mode.
A request has already been pre-allocated and transferred, queueing to be added

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix --config arg expansion called from api_server.py
vllm.author: dubejf
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Per --help, the argument parser for the vllm CLI and the api_server entry point both accept the --config command-line argument, but only the CLI handles it correctly. This PR ensures consistent config file handling for all entry points.
Examples:


vllm serve --config config.yaml
